- The Problem:
    Windows Explorer caches disk space values. After running cleanup scripts, Explorer shows stale (old) free space numbers until you restart it
    or reboot.
    The Fix:

    1. Added refresh-disk function that:
        - Kills and restarts explorer.exe (forces cache flush)
        - Clears icon cache files
        - Runs garbage collection to flush filesystem buffers
        - Shows accurate disk space via WMI (not cached PowerShell drives)
    2. Updated ccc1 from:
        function ccc1 { siz; mgr; ccsizes; cleanc; siz }
    3. To:
        function ccc1 { siz; mgr; ccsizes; cleanc; refresh-disk }

    Key change: The final siz used Get-PSDrive which can show cached values. Now refresh-disk restarts Explorer and uses Get-WmiObject
    Win32_LogicalDisk which reads directly from the filesystem.
    To verify it works:
    refresh-disk
    Or run your full cleanup:
    ccc1
    After ccc1 completes, open "This PC" in Explorer - the free space should now match what was reported in the terminal.
